title: Define Signature
text: Define Signature Use the Signature Editor (Define Signature) to create or edit the signatures for Uniface and non-Uniface components. To compile a signature, choose File >Compile Signature . Buttons Allow Edit —enable entity-level signatures to be edited or deleted. Note: We recommend that you do not use the Signature Editor to manage entity-level signatures. By default, it is not possible to modify or delete entity-level signatures. If you must do so, first click Allow Edit . Properties —define signature properties, including the call-in properties of a component. + —add an operation or parameter to the signature >> —display and other dialog box in which you can select an item or define additional properties Fields Description Implementations Comments Operation Comments (tab) Pre-Conditions Post-Conditions Return Value Parameter In/Out Data Type For Uniface components, the Description and Comments fields are not overwritten when the component is compiled using the component editor. How to Define a Component Signature To define the properties of a signature, click the Properties button. This also enables you to maintain the call-in properties of a component. To modify entity-level signatures, click Allow Edit , then choose the desired action from the File or Edit menu. (When performing global updates, it is not necessary to first click Allow Edit .)
